ques: Identify the latest health-related news on BBC News and summarize the main findings or recommendations.
answer: BBC News Health's latest top story, "Want to feel happier at work? Take a five-minute walk," reports a new British Journal of Sports Medicine study (Columbia University, 11,000+ office workers) finding that a five-minute walking break every hour is the most feasible way to improve mood, alertness and productivity. Prolonged sitting raises risks of obesity, heart disease and type 2 diabetes, so the recommendation is to take hourly "movement snacks" (e.g. walking meetings or pacing during calls) — though researchers caution the data was self-reported and short-term.
